cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when David could have gone to the football field. The problem gives me several alibis from different people, and I need to see when David was not accounted for. The key is to check the timeline and find the time gaps where he wasn't doing any of these activities. Also, remember that the football field closes after 10pm, so he must have gone before that. First, let me list out all the times David was seen doing other things: 1. David woke up at 5am. So before 5am, he was asleep. But the first activity starts at 5am. 2. Steven saw David buying a phone from 5am to 6am. 3. Kimberly saw David at the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 6am to 8am. 4. Betty saw David working out at the gym from 9am to 12pm. 5. Lisa saw David driving to the water park from 12pm to 3pm. 6. William saw David on a rooftop from 3pm to 10pm. Now, let me map these out in order to see the time gaps: - 5am to 6am: buying a phone (Steven) - 6am to 8am: photos at Pisa (Kimberly) - 8am to 9am: ??? (This is a gap between Kimberly's sighting and Betty's gym time) - 9am to 12pm: gym (Betty) - 12pm to 3pm: driving to water park (Lisa) - 3pm to 10pm: rooftop (William) So between 8am and 9am, David isn't reported to be doing anything. That seems like the only time window when he wasn't seen by anyone. Also, after 10pm the football field is closed, so he couldn't have gone then even if he was free. Looking at the options: (A) 6am to 8am – No, that's when he was at
